history.text: Luxembourg became independent from the French Empire and elevation to Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, marking a key step in modern statehood.
Independence in personal Union with the Netherlands (Treaty of London), a key milestone in Luxembourg's path toward modern sovereignty tied the territory into a wider political structure.
End of personal union with the Kingdom of the Netherlands ruled or strongly influenced the territory before the modern state.
Liberation from the Greater German Reich restored control after occupation.

worthSeeing.title: Luxembourg City Fortress:
worthSeeing.text: The UNESCO old quarters and fortifications on dramatic sandstone cliffs.

worthSeeing.title: Mullerthal Trail:
worthSeeing.text: Sandstone rock formations and forest paths in the "Little Switzerland" of Luxembourg.

worthSeeing.title: Moselle Wine Valley:
worthSeeing.text: Luxembourg's wine country along the Moselle river, bordering Germany.
